ud8.8:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at the Eastern Monastery, the palace of Migāra’s mother.
ud8.8:1.3: And on that occasion a dear and beloved grandson of Visākhā, Migāra’s mother, had died.
ud8.8:1.4:
So Visākhā, Migāra’s mother–her clothes wet, her hair wet–
went to the Blessed One in the middle of the day and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud8.8:1.5: As she was sitting there the Blessed One said to her:
ud8.8:2.1:
“Why have you come here, Visākhā–your clothes wet, your hair wet–in the middle of the day?”
When this was said, she said to the Blessed One,
ud8.8:2.2: “My dear and beloved grandson has died.
ud8.8:2.3: This is why I have come here–my clothes wet, my hair wet–in the middle of the day.”
ud8.8:2.4: “Visākhā, would you like to have as many children & grandchildren as there are people in Sāvatthī?”
ud8.8:2.5: “Yes, lord, I would like to have as many children & grandchildren as there are people in Sāvatthī.”
ud8.8:3.1: “But how many people in Sāvatthī die in the course of a day?”
ud8.8:3.2: “Sometimes ten people die in Sāvatthī in the course of a day, sometimes nine…
ud8.8:3.11: Sometimes one person dies in Sāvatthī in the course of a day.
ud8.8:3.12: Sāvatthī is never free from people dying.”
ud8.8:4.1:
“So what do you think, Visākhā?
Would you ever be free of wet clothes & wet hair?”
ud8.8:4.3: Enough of my having so many children & grandchildren.”
ud8.8:5.1: “Visākhā, those who have a hundred dear ones have a hundred sufferings.
ud8.8:5.2: Those who have ninety dear ones have ninety sufferings.
ud8.8:5.19: Those who have one dear one have one suffering.
ud8.8:5.20:
Those who have no dear ones have no sufferings.
They are free from sorrow, free from stain, free from lamentation, I tell you.”
ud8.8:6.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.8:7.2: the many kinds of suffering in the world,
ud8.8:7.4: They don’t exist when there’s nothing dear.
ud8.8:8.2: are those for whom nothing in the world is anywhere dear.
ud8.8:8.3: So one who aspires to the stainless & sorrowless
ud8.8:8.4: shouldn’t make anything dear in the world anywhere.
